# OpenScout

OpenScout is a local-first control plane for AI agents. It gives Claude Code,
Codex, and future harnesses one shared broker/runtime/protocol so agents can be
discovered, addressed, observed, and composed without every surface inventing a
separate state model.

Current posture: high-trust local developer pilots. Not enterprise-ready,
compliance-ready, or a hardened multi-tenant runtime. License posture is not yet
finalized; package manifests currently use `UNLICENSED`.

## Fast Context

- Runtime: `packages/runtime`
- Protocol: `packages/protocol`
- Desktop/CLI: `apps/desktop`
- iOS: `apps/ios`
- Web package: `packages/web`
- Public CLI package: `packages/cli`
- Docs: `docs`

## Start Here

- `README.md` — repo overview and bootstrap
- `docs/quickstart.md` — first healthy local run
- `docs/current-posture.md` — maturity, trust, mesh, install footprint, and license boundaries
- `docs/architecture.md` — broker/runtime/protocol model
- `docs/data-ownership.md` — Scout-owned records vs observed harness material
- `docs/agent-integration-contract.md` — minimum contract for agents and adapters
- `docs/agent-identity.md` — address grammar and resolution
- `docs/collaboration-workflows-v1.md` — question vs work item model
- `docs/operator-attention-and-unblock.md` — approvals, permissions, human-input notifications

## Agent-Optimized Docs

- `llms-full.txt` — larger generated context bundle for copy/paste into agents
- `install.md` — install/bootstrap guide with success criteria and support footprint
- `docs/agent/README.agent.md`
- `docs/agent/current-posture.agent.md`
- `docs/agent/integration-contract.agent.md`

## Core Rules

- The broker is the canonical writer for Scout-owned coordination records.
- Scout owns messages, invocations, flights, deliveries, bindings, agent registrations, questions, and work items created through Scout.
- Scout observes external harness transcripts such as Claude Code and Codex JSONL; do not bulk-import those turns as first-party Scout messages.
- Mesh means reachability and coordination, not exactly-once delivery, global consensus, or transcript replication.
- Use `scout send` / `messages_send` for tell/update.
- Use `scout ask` / `invocations_ask` for owned work or requested replies.
- Use explicit target fields; do not hide routing in message body text.

## Verification Commands

```bash
bun run --cwd apps/desktop check
npm --prefix packages/runtime run check
npm --prefix packages/protocol run check
```

Root `npm run check` may fail if a workspace uses a bare `tsc` binary that is
not installed in the current shell environment. Prefer package-local checks when
working in a specific area.
